summary: Gruber highlights a hidden feature of Apple's new Remote app for iPhone: it provides a proper keyboard for typing on Apple TV. The on-screen Apple TV keyboard is clunky, using an alphabetical layout navigated by directional buttons on the physical remote. The iPhone Remote app automatically presents a keyboard whenever text input is needed on Apple TV, with characters appearing live on screen. Gruber notes this useful feature appears to be undocumented by Apple.
key_points: Apple TV's built-in on-screen keyboard uses a cumbersome ABCDEF layout navigated by directional buttons
The iPhone Remote app automatically offers a proper keyboard whenever Apple TV requires text input
Characters typed on the iPhone are reflected live on the Apple TV screen
Apple apparently hasn't promoted this as a feature of the Remote app
money_quotes: One thing that stinks about Apple TV is the on-screen keyboard.

You don't need to type often, but when you do — like for searching YouTube, or entering a Wi-Fi password — you get a crummy on-screen keyboard (ABCDEF rather than QWERTY) that you have to navigate using the up/down/left/right buttons on the little remote.
A nice little touch, and, as far as I can tell, something Apple hasn't mentioned as one of the Remote app's features.
key_insight: Apple's Remote app quietly solves one of Apple TV's worst interaction problems by letting users type on their iPhone instead of navigating a clunky on-screen keyboard.
